title: AIIMS BSc Nursing Exam Date 2026: full schedule, registration, eligibility & syllabus

detail.title: AIIMS BSc Nursing Exam Date 2026: full schedule, registration, eligibility & syllabus
detail.short_info: AIIMS BSc Nursing Exam Date 2026 has been announced for BSc Nursing (Hons) and Post-Basic courses on the official website - aiimsexams.ac.in. Check the detailed schedule, exam pattern, and direct link to download the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date notice and admit card, along with steps to check the AIIMS BSc Nursing result 2026 online.

sections.heading: AIIMS BSc Nursing Exam Date 2026: full schedule, registration, eligibility & syllabus

content.text: The AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 has been scheduled for 27 June 2026 for the BSc Nursing (Hons) course as per the latest AIIMS Delhi exam calendar and leading education portals. If you are targeting AIIMS, this one exam can literally decide your nursing career, so having all the dates and details in one place really helps.

sections.heading: AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 (Hons & Post-Basic)

content.text: According to the tentative schedule of entrance examinations released on the AIIMS official site and current academic updates, the AIIMS BSc Nursing (Hons) exam is planned for 27 June 2026 (Saturday) in computer-based mode. Many reputed exam portals also mention 30 May 2026 as the exam date for AIIMS BSc Nursing (Post-Basic) Stage 1, so candidates for both courses should mark these dates carefully.
Do remember that AIIMS sometimes labels these dates as “tentative”, so it’s always better to double‑check the latest notice on the official AIIMS exams website before planning travel or leave.

Exact day‑wise details like the last time for fee payment or city choice will be clearly mentioned in the official AIIMS notification PDF on aiimsexams.ac.in, so always cross‑check before the deadline.

Through the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 schedule, AIIMS Delhi and other AIIMS institutes offer admission to undergraduate nursing programmes at different levels. The main programmes under AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 are listed below.

items: BSc (Hons) Nursing
BSc Nursing (Post-Basic)

content.text: Seat numbers and AIIMS‑wise distribution are released in the official prospectus every year, so it’s a good habit to read that document once before filling choices.

Before worrying about the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026, you need to be sure you actually satisfy the eligibility criteria for your category and course. While the official notice will give the final word, the general eligibility pattern for AIIMS BSc Nursing (Hons) usually looks like this.

items: Indian nationals are eligible to apply for AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026.
Some seats may also be available for OCI / foreign nationals as per AIIMS rules (check the latest prospectus).
Female candidates are primarily eligible for BSc Nursing (Hons), although AIIMS notifications sometimes specify institute‑wise rules, so check carefully.
Minimum age is usually 17 years as on 31 December 2026.
There is generally no upper age limit for AIIMS BSc Nursing (Hons), but candidates should confirm this in the 2026 brochure once released.
Class 12 or equivalent from a recognised board with Physics, Chemistry, Biology and English.
Minimum aggregate marks are typically around 55% for General / OBC and 50% for SC / ST categories (subject to official confirmation for 2026).

content.text: For BSc Nursing (Post-Basic), candidates usually need a valid 10+2 plus a registered Nursing qualification (GNM) along with registration as a nurse and midwife with the State Nursing Council.

AIIMS conducts the entire AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 application process in online mode via its PAAR portal, so there is no offline form or printed application. The process is transparent but slightly long, so doing it slowly on a laptop rather than rushing on a mobile is a good idea.

items: Visit the official AIIMS exams website aiimsexams.ac.in and go to the BSc Nursing 2026 section.
Complete Basic Registration under the PAAR system by entering personal details and uploading your photo, signature and thumb impression in the correct format.
After acceptance of basic registration, proceed for Final Registration, where you will fill academic details, select exam city, and pay the application fee online.
Verify all details before submitting; mistakes in category, name or date of birth can cause issues later in counselling.

content.text: The application fee for AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 is usually similar to other AIIMS UG exams and is paid online via debit card, credit card or net banking.
The exact AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 fee amounts will be clearly mentioned in the official advertisement and should always be checked from there only to avoid confusion.

The AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 is fixed for a single shift, and the test is conducted entirely in CBT mode at designated centres across India. Knowing the exam pattern in advance helps you plan a realistic attempt strategy, especially if you are weak in any one subject.

rows: +1 for correct, -0.33 for incorrect, 0 for unattempted
English (and sometimes Hindi, as per AIIMS notice)

content.text: There is negative marking, so random guessing on every single question can pull your score down instead of helping, especially if your accuracy is low.

Although the AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 syllabus is not very different from your Class 11 and 12 NCERT topics, the level of questions can be slightly tricky. Most of the paper focuses on Physics, Chemistry, Biology, and sometimes General Knowledge or English depending on the year’s pattern.

items: Physics: Mechanics, kinematics, work, energy, power, heat and thermodynamics, waves, optics, electrostatics, current electricity, modern physics.
Chemistry: Atomic structure, chemical bonding, states of matter, thermodynamics, equilibrium, redox, solutions, organic chemistry basics, biomolecules.
Biology: Cell structure, cell division, genetics, evolution, human physiology, plant physiology, reproduction, ecology, human health and diseases.
General Knowledge / English (if applicable): Basic GK, current affairs related to health and science, comprehension, vocabulary and basic grammar.

content.text: AIIMS generally sticks close to the NCERT level for core topics, so finishing NCERTs properly is still one of the best strategies even in 2026.

sections.heading: AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 Admit Card and Exam day guidelines

content.text: Around one week before the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026, AIIMS will release the admit card for all eligible candidates on its official website. Candidates have to download it themselves; AIIMS does not send any hard copy by post.

items: Log in using your registration ID and password and download the admit card in PDF form.
Check your name, photo, exam date, shift timing, and test centre address carefully.
Carry a printed copy of the admit card along with a valid photo ID like Aadhaar, PAN, driving licence or passport.
Reach the centre at least 60-90 minutes before reporting time to avoid last‑minute panic and document issues.

content.text: It’s also better not to carry unnecessary items like books, notes or electronic gadgets, since AIIMS centres are usually strict about barred items.

sections.heading: AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 result, cut-off and counselling

content.text: After the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 is over, AIIMS declares the result in online mode only, usually within a few weeks.The result may be released as a scorecard and sometimes as a merit list with roll numbers and category‑wise cut‑off scores

items: Login to your AIIMS account and download the AIIMS BSc Nursing 2026 result or scorecard.
Check your marks, percentile and All India Rank (AIR) along with category rank if provided.
Separate merit lists are usually prepared for UR, OBC, SC, ST, EWS and PwBD candidates.
Qualified candidates must register for online counselling on the AIIMS portal as per the schedule.
You will fill your choices of AIIMS institutes and courses in the given time window.
Seat allotment is done based on rank, category, preferences and seat availability.
Once a seat is allotted, you need to download the allotment letter and report to the respective AIIMS with original documents for verification.

content.text: AIIMS may also allow upgradation in further rounds, so if you are not happy with your first‑round seat, you can still opt for a better choice in the next round while following the official rules.

sections.heading: Preparation tips for AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026

content.text: With the AIIMS BSc Nursing exam date 2026 already fixed in June, you actually know the exact number of months and days you have left to prepare. A slightly disciplined but flexible plan can easily push you above the cut‑off if you start on time.

items: Finish NCERT Class 11 and 12 for Physics, Chemistry and Biology thoroughly before jumping into any advanced books.
Practice AIIMS nursing previous year questions and mock tests to understand question framing and time pressure.
Make a small formula and diagram notebook for last‑week revision instead of reading entire chapters again.
Keep one day every week only for revision and test analysis rather than starting new topics endlessly.

content.text: Also, take good care of your sleep in the last week before 27 June 2026, because two hours of focused mind in the exam hall will beat months of half‑sleepy study any day.
